steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Apply the triangle area formula
steps.content: The area of a triangle depends on its base and height. We have base = 54 cm and height = 84 cm.

steps.title: Substitute and calculate the area
steps.content:
Substitute base = 54 and height = 84 into the formula:
$$A = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 54 \cdot 84$$
Simplify by calculating $\frac{1}{2} \cdot 54 = 27$:
$$A = 27 \cdot 84 = 2,268$$
The area is 2,268 square centimeters. That matches Choice A — 2,268.
